markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Paris be 14°C or higher on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Météo-France and ECMWF ensemble forecasts indicate Paris overnight lows clustering tightly around 10–11°C on May 1 under a high-pressure ridge promoting radiative cooling via mostly clear skies, driving the near-even trader split between these outcomes at 33–35% implied probabilities. Differentiating factors include model variances in boundary layer mixing from light southerly winds (5–10 km/h) and residual soil moisture from recent April showers, with ECMWF leaning slightly cooler than GFS due to deeper inversion layers; urban heat island effects at official stations like Paris-Montsouris could nudge toward 11°C. Historical May 1 averages sit at 9°C, but 2026's positive temperature anomalies from a mild spring elevate expectations. New 12z model runs today may refine consensus ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Ren vs. Hercog: Match O/U 23.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yufei Ren and Polona Hercog in the Huzhou, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 11:00PM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Huzhou: Yufei Ren vs Polona Hercog Match O/U 23.5

events.title: Huzhou: Yufei Ren vs Polona Hercog
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yufei Ren and Polona Hercog in the Huzhou, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 11:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Yufei Ren' if Yufei Ren advances against Polona Hercog.

This market will resolve to 'Polona Hercog' if Polona Hercog advances against Yufei Ren.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polona Hercog enters the Huzhou Open round of 16 with momentum after upsetting eighth seed Xinyu Gao 4-6, 7-6(5), 6-3 on red clay in the round of 32, showcasing her veteran experience and clay-court affinity at age 35 and ranked No. 249. Yufei Ren, the 20-year-old Chinese home favorite ranked No. 368, advanced convincingly 6-2, 6-1 over Yidi Yang, riding a strong 24-7 year-to-date record amid limited higher-level exposure. With no head-to-head history, trader consensus weighs Hercog's higher ranking and stylistic edge on the slower surface against Ren's youth, crowd support, and fresher legs from a straight-sets win, in this WTA 125 encounter under mild spring conditions.

events.title: Clube do Remo vs. SE Palmeiras
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Clube do Remo and SE Palmeiras.

eventMetadata.context_description: Palmeiras' strong second-place standing in Serie A, with 22 goals already amid solid away form (D W W L W), drives trader consensus to a 57% implied probability for victory over struggling 16th-placed Remo, who sit low on 13 goals despite recent home resilience including a 0-0 draw against Grêmio and a 4-1 upset win over Bahia. Remo's Baenão fortress boosts draw pricing to 26.5%, reflecting their L D W L D home streak and potential to frustrate via defensive setup. Ongoing Palmeiras injuries—Piquerez (ankle surgery), Vitor Roque (ankle), Jefté (thigh)—temper dominance without derailing favoritism, while limited head-to-head history leaves room for Remo's 21.5% upset potential in Belém's humid conditions.

markets.question: Will JD Vance say "King" or "Queen" on the Will Cain Show?
markets.description:
JD Vance is scheduled to appear on the Will Cain Show on April 29, 2026. You can read more about that here: https://x.com/WillCainShow/status/2049245211012870401

This market will resolve to "Yes" if JD Vance says the listed term during this appearance. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Vance is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this interview is definitively cancelled by JD Vance or the Will Cain Show, or otherwise is not released by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If the interview has not aired in its entirety by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on what has aired up to that point.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution. Transcripts will not count.

markets.question: Amiens SC vs. Red Star FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 2 game between Amiens SC and Red Star FC, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Amiens SC and Red Star FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue2.fr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Amiens SC vs. Red Star FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amiens SC, mired in 18th place in Ligue 2 with a dismal home record featuring just two wins from 15 matches, hosts fifth-placed Red Star FC on May 2 in a crucial relegation scrap. Amiens' recent form underscores their woes—a 0-1 home defeat to Pau FC on April 10, a 1-1 draw at Bastia three days prior, and ongoing absences of attacking midfielder Victor Lobry (cruciate ligament tear since November) and left-back Arvin Appiah (Achilles injury). Red Star enters with momentum from a 4-3 win over Bastia, solidifying their playoff push despite Amiens' 3-1 victory in the reverse fixture last August. Head-to-head favors Amiens (7 wins in 13), but Red Star's superior goal difference and depth tilt trader consensus amid late-season stakes.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: INFINITE enters this United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket BO3 as the clear trader consensus favorite, bolstered by their #65 HLTV world ranking, recent United21 League Season 46 title, Parken Challenger Championship Season 5 win, and four-match streak prior to the April 9 matchup. Imperial Academy, at #224 HLTV, earned their spot topping Group A undefeated (2-0, +16 map differential) against weaker foes like LPH Gaming and Sangal ALTERS, but posted a 37.5% win rate in the prior three months with losses to HEROIC Academy and Hashiras. INFINITE's group stage (2-1 in Group D) featured stand-ins like SHiPZ and marTineZ, yet core riflers kreaz, Blytz, mhN1, Dytor, and sl3nd provide stylistic edges in pistol rounds and clutches on vetoed maps like Ancient and Mirage.

events.title: Mexico vs. South Africa
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Thursday, June 11, 2026 between Mexico and South Africa.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico's status as 2026 FIFA World Cup co-host and superior FIFA ranking of 15th versus South Africa's 60th underpin the 66% implied probability for an El Tri victory in their Group A opener at high-altitude Estadio Azteca. Recent April rankings confirm this gap, while Mexico's dominant 4-0 friendly win over Iceland bolsters trader confidence despite ongoing injury woes—midfielder Marcel Ruiz out with a torn ACL, goalkeeper Ángel Malagón sidelined by Achilles rupture, and defender Julián Araujo's recent knock adding uncertainty under coach Javier Aguirre. South Africa's 14.5% underdog pricing reflects their recent 1-2 friendly loss to Panama and historical head-to-head parity (1-1 in 2010 World Cup), making a draw at 20.5% a prudent trader hedge amid opener caution.

markets.question: Will Gold (XAUUSD) hit (HIGH) $5,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for Gold (XAUUSD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session as listed on Pyth will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Gold (XAUUSD) does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Gold (XAUUSD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Metal.XAU%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the relevant CME COMEX futures contract for the underlying metal—COMEX Gold Futures (GC)—may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit in April 2026?
events.description: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit in April 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold prices hover near $4,570 per ounce amid trader caution following the Federal Reserve's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% for a third meeting, despite March 2026 CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024. A firmer U.S. dollar index around 98.6 and rising Treasury yields have pressured the non-yielding asset, exacerbating a pullback from earlier 2026 highs above $4,800, while surging oil prices from U.S.-Iran tensions bolster safe-haven bids. Central bank purchases, led by Poland's 20+ tonnes added this year, provide underlying support. Watch April CPI release on May 10 and May nonfarm payrolls for shifts in rate cut expectations.

markets.question: X banned in any European country by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the use of X/Twitter is banned within any European country by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a “European country” is defined as any of the following sovereign states: Albania, Andorra, Austria, Belarus, Belgium, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Georgia, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Kosovo, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Moldova, Monaco, Montenegro, Netherlands, North Macedonia, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, San Marino, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, Ukraine, United Kingdom, and Vatican City.

A ban will qualify if legislation is enacted or government action is taken to bar the respective country's citizens from downloading and/or viewing X/Twitter, and/or posting on X/Twitter. Any legislation or government action that meets these standards will qualify, regardless of whether or when the ban goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the respective government and X/Twitter; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 75.5% implied probability for "No" on X facing a ban in any European country by year-end, driven by the platform's aggressive legal challenge to the European Commission's €120 million Digital Services Act (DSA) fine issued in December 2025 for transparency lapses around blue-check verification. X met a March 2026 compliance deadline by submitting a remediation plan, signaling willingness to address content moderation and data access issues without full capitulation, while escalating the dispute to Europe's top court. Despite polls showing public support for potential bans amid ongoing DSA probes—including Paris office raids—no member state has pursued outright prohibition, as regulators prioritize escalating fines (up to 6% of global turnover) over drastic suspensions. Key catalysts ahead include court rulings on the fine and further enforcement deadlines, though historical DSA patterns favor penalties over platform bans.

eventMetadata.context_description: Early in the 2026 Eliteserien season, trader consensus prices Sandefjord, Fredrikstad, Bodø/Glimt, and Kristiansund as virtual co-favorites at 42-46% implied probabilities for the title, underscoring a tightly contested race where current table leader Tromsø's 19 points from eight matches (six wins, one draw, stout 14:2 goal difference) garners just 30% due to skepticism over sustaining northern home dominance amid travel fatigue. Sandefjord (seventh, 7 points from six: two wins, one draw, three losses, 4:7 GD) and Fredrikstad (ninth/tenth similarly, 8:12 GD) have impressed with recent clean sheets and gritty draws against top sides, fueling upset potential despite modest starts. Bodø/Glimt (fifth, 10 points from five/six) rebounds from Viking's 5-0 thrashing on April 11 via historical pedigree as multi-time champions, while Kristiansund's (eighth, 7 points) wins over Brann and Fredrikstad keep them competitive; with 30+ rounds left, fixture difficulty, injuries, and home/away form will dictate shifts.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Green Party candidate Zoë Garbett at 85% implied probability to win the Hackney mayoral election on May 7, driven by recent MRP polling from JL Partners showing Greens leading Labour 40-33% borough-wide—up 17 points for Greens since 2022—alongside projections of Green council control with 29 of 57 seats. Incumbent Labour mayor Caroline Woodley trails at 15.5%, reflecting Labour's sharp vote decline amid national party challenges and recent by-election losses to Greens, such as September 2024's Stoke Newington gain. Key local issues like housing shortages and cost-of-living pressures amplify the Green surge in this first-past-the-post race, though turnout and late shifts could influence the closely watched outcome. Minor candidates from Conservative, Liberal Democrats, and Reform UK hold negligible shares due to fragmented support.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Angers, drawing 1-1 with Le Havre on April 18, grapple with a depleted defense: Yassin Belkhdim (arm fracture, season over), Harouna Djibirin (ligament, out long-term), Carlens Arcus (groin), and Marius Courcoul absent, Haris Belkebla doubtful per April 16 updates. markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at $2.00-$3.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Apr 27 – May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

markets.question: Will Anthropic have the highest estimated revenue for Apr 27–May 3, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the model family that has the highest total estimated inference revenue based on Anera's data published at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family for the specified week.

The week’s values will be determined by summing the respective companies' estimated revenue figures for each day within the specified period. If data for all specified days is not released by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. Entries labeled “Other” will not be considered a model family for the purposes of this market. Revisions to published data will be considered only until all relevant data for the specified period has been released.

Model families will be ranked primarily by their total estimated inference revenue over the specified seven-day period, with alphabetical order of model family names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if two model families are exactly tied, “Anthropic” would be ranked ahead of “OpenAI”). This market will resolve based on the model family that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is data published by Anera Intelligence at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family. If the relevant data for the full period is not available by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. If this resolution source is unavailable, this market will remain open until the data becomes available and will resolve based on the first subsequent check.

markets.question: Will the Ethiopian Citizens for Social Justice (EZEMA) win the most seats in the 2026 Ethiopian general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Ethiopian House of Peoples' Representatives election. 

If the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Ethiopian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Ethiopian government, specifically the National Election Board of Ethiopia (NEBE) (nebe.org.et/en).

Scenarios challenging this include widespread violence prompting delays, major scandals, or unified opposition surge in key constituencies, though historical patterns suggest low upset risk.

markets.question: Will Gold (XAUUSD) hit (HIGH) $5,050 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Gold (XAUUSD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session as listed on Pyth will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Gold (XAUUSD) does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Gold (XAUUSD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Metal.XAU%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the relevant CME COMEX futures contract for the underlying metal—COMEX Gold Futures (GC)—may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit Week of April 27 2026?
events.description: What will Gold (XAUUSD) hit Week of April 27 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Gold (XAUUSD) reflects a post-FOMC pullback, with spot prices retreating from weekly highs near $4,702 on April 27 to around $4,560 per ounce as of April 30 amid the Federal Reserve's April 28-29 decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3½-3¾ percent for the third consecutive meeting. Solid economic expansion, persistent March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, and four dissenting votes for cuts bolstered the U.S. dollar index to 98.6 while lifting 10-year Treasury yields toward 4.4%, pressuring the non-yielding asset lower despite ongoing geopolitical tensions including the Iran conflict. Traders eye tomorrow's May 1 nonfarm payrolls and May 12 April CPI release as pivotal for rate path expectations, with Polymarket aggregating real-capital consensus around sub-$4,700 resolution for the week.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 73% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, reflecting stalled momentum since the company's confidential S-1 filing in January with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan, which targeted a March debut at a $15 billion-plus valuation but yielded no public prospectus or roadshow amid softening secondary market pricing around $7–10 billion—down from 2021's $15 billion peak. The 20% odds on a sub-$15 billion market cap if listed align with estimated $800 million annual recurring revenue and recent profitability, though enterprise expansion challenges persist in a competitive communication sector. With two months to resolution, watch for S-1 emergence or withdrawal amid volatile tech IPO sentiment.

markets.question: Will Baidu have the second best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the Kharg Island oil terminal be hit by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Kharg Island oil terminal is the subject of a kinetic strike by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying strikes occurring on or after February 28 ET will count even if they occurred before market creation.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will the Kharg Island oil terminal be hit by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Kharg Island oil terminal is the subject of a kinetic strike between February 28 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Qualifying strikes occurring on or after February 28 ET will count even if they occurred before market creation.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Kharg Island oil terminal be hit

eventMetadata.context_description: US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has intensified economic pressure on Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal, which handles 90% of the country's crude exports, without direct strikes on oil infrastructure. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated on April 29 that storage there nears capacity, potentially forcing production cuts and $170 million daily revenue losses within days, reflecting trader consensus on low escalation risk to the terminal itself. Prior US airstrikes targeted only military sites on the island (latest April 7), amid President Trump's threats of seizure or destruction that have not materialized. No verified kinetic attacks on the terminal occurred in the past 30 days; market resolves April 30 on any such event by 11:59 PM ET, with de-escalation signals prevailing despite ongoing conflict.

markets.question: Will Mark Dixon win the 2026 Watford mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Watford mayoral election is currently scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of Watford as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the Watford Borough Council.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Liberal Democrat Mayor Peter Taylor commands 93.5% trader consensus in the Watford Mayoral Election set for May 7, driven by his strong re-election bid following a March 30 manifesto launch emphasizing local delivery and a decade-plus of party dominance under first-past-the-post voting. Fragmented opposition—six challengers including Labour's Keith Morgan (3.1%), Conservative Abdul Laskar (1.4%), and independents—splits anti-incumbent votes, reinforcing his lead amid recent hustings showcasing council achievements. While odds imply near-certainty reflecting skin-in-the-game wisdom of crowds, low-turnout surprises, a late scandal, or unified challenger momentum could narrow the gap before polls close.

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: Will Giannis Antetokounmpo play for the Atlanta Hawks in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team Giannis Antetokounmpo officially joins by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo does not officially join a new team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Milwaukee Bucks”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Giannis Antetokounmpo retires or is not under contract with any professional team by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official acquisition announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the Milwaukee Bucks and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane tops trader consensus for the 2026 Ballon d'Or at 36% implied probability, propelled by his sensational 53-goal haul in 45 Bayern Munich outings—including multiple hat-tricks—during their Bundesliga title clinch on April 19. Teammate Michael Olise follows at 16.5%, buoyed by 22 goals and 27 assists in a breakout campaign, with Oliver Kahn hailing his Champions League exploits against PSG as Ballon d'Or caliber. Kylian Mbappé's 12% reflects Real Madrid's form wobbles despite strong numbers, Ousmane Dembélé holds 11.1% on PSG's Ligue 1 surge, and Lamine Yamal slips to 8.5% post-injury, as the UCL knockout stages and World Cup loom to reshape the race.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's dominant 99.5% implied probability in the Bundesliga top goalscorer market stems from his insurmountable 33-goal haul through matchday 31, a 15-goal lead over nearest challenger Deniz Undav's 18 for VfB Stuttgart, with only three matchdays remaining. Kane's recent form, including goals in Bayern Munich's title-clinching 4-2 win over Stuttgart on April 19 and a late winner in the 4-3 thriller at Mainz on April 25, has widened the gap further amid Bayern's secured championship. Challengers like Serhou Guirassy and Luis Díaz (both 15 goals) face mathematical impossibility, even scoring every remaining hat-trick, while Mohammed Amoura's eight goals for Wolfsburg explain his slight 0.5% edge among longshots. Only a freak scenario—Kane suffering a season-ending injury with no further play—could theoretically challenge, though ties resolve by assists or appearances.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit Academy enters this Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 BO3 matchup as the higher-ranked squad, backed by their parent organization's resources and a 3-1 Contenders Stage qualification featuring strong wins on Nuke and Overpass. Their roster, including riflers VILBy and mazay alongside AWPer Kiryasoo, has shown consistent map control in recent European online events, building momentum toward a tournament runner-up finish. Wave Esports, qualifying 3-1 from Contenders despite a weaker overall profile (world #516), fields stand-ins cMS and pxsl amid March losses at Urban Riga Open and Esplay Backforce Masters. Traders weigh Spirit's firepower edge against Wave's upset potential in swiss format volatility, with standard CS2 map pool (Dust2, Mirage, Ancient played) favoring experienced executes.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 240-259 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $6,200 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures (GC) have pulled back to around $4,580 per ounce following the Federal Reserve's April 2026 FOMC meeting, where policymakers held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid sticky inflation evidenced by March CPI rising 3.3% year-over-year, diminishing rate cut expectations and lifting 10-year Treasury yields to 4.36% alongside a firmer U.S. Dollar Index near 98.6—key headwinds for the non-yielding metal. Central bank purchases and geopolitical tensions offer counterbalance, though ETF outflows signal cooling investor demand. Traders eye April CPI on May 12, May employment data June 5, and the June 16-17 FOMC for catalysts that could shift monetary policy outlook and gold's trajectory by month-end.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the best Coding AI model at the end of April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Zamalek SC vs. El Ahly SC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Egypt Egyptian Premier League game between Zamalek SC and El Ahly SC, scheduled for May 1 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Zamalek SC and El Ahly SC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efa.com.eg. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Recent surges fuel the tightness: Chris Gotterup's WM Phoenix Open playoff win over Hideki Matsuyama vaulted both to 17.5%, Sepp Straka and Adam Scott match there on consistent form, while Collin Morikawa's precise irons (18%) and Rory McIlroy's repeat Masters victory (14%) loom large. Matt Fitzpatrick's RBC Heritage win bolsters the points lead, underscoring the wide-open FedEx Cup grind with months left.

markets.question: Will Demeke Mekonnen be the next Prime Minister of Ethiopia?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Ethiopia following the 2026 General elections.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Ethiopia. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Ethiopia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed commands 90.5% trader consensus as the Prosperity Party's unchallenged leader ahead of Ethiopia's June 1, 2026, general election for the House of Peoples' Representatives, which elects the premier. His party's supermajority from the 2021 vote, coupled with institutional control and dissolution of major rivals, underpins this dominance despite persistent Amhara and Oromia conflicts and criticisms of electoral fairness. Recent initiatives, including green heavy machinery policies and AU AI Champion appointment, signal continuity. Upsets could arise from opposition surges, election postponement due to insecurity, internal party fractures, or late scandals, though these face steep barriers given historical incumbency advantages.

markets.question: Israel x Hamas ceasefire cancelled by June 30?
markets.description:
On October 9, 2025 Israel and Hamas signed a deal implementing a ceasefire: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israel-hamas-agree-gaza-ceasefire-return-hostages-2025-10-09/

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a either Israel or Hamas announce the cancellation of the ongoing ceasefire, or if a consensus of credible reporting confirms that the ceasefire is no longer in effect, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements that one side or another violated the ongoing ceasefire will not be enough to resolve this market to "Yes"; only a definitive announcement by Israel or Hamas, or otherwise a wide consensus of credible reporting that that the ceasefire has been ended, will qualify to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary solution resolution source will be official information from Hamas or the government of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US-brokered Israel-Hamas ceasefire, in effect since October 2025, remains increasingly fragile six months on, as UN officials noted on April 28 amid ongoing Israeli strikes killing at least 32 Palestinians since early April and IDF reports of five Hamas violations between April 17-21, including armed activities. Deadlock centers on Hamas disarmament and Gaza governance, with the group signaling willingness to relinquish some weapons but refusing full compliance, fueling trader concerns over escalation. Regional de-escalation via Israel-Lebanon and Israel-Iran truces has refocused attention on Gaza's humanitarian crisis, where nearly all residents remain displaced; upcoming Israeli elections by October 27 could intensify pressures on Netanyahu's coalition to act decisively.

markets.question: Map Handicap: NAVI (-1.5) vs Karmine Corp (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Natus Vincere and Karmine Corp in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere wins 2 or more maps than Karmine Corp in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Karmine Corp". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: NAVI (-1.5) vs Karmine Corp (+1.5)

events.title: Valorant: Natus Vincere vs Karmine Corp (BO3) - VCT EMEA Group Alpha
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Natus Vincere and Karmine Corp in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Karmine Corp.

This market will resolve to "Karmine Corp" if Karmine Corp win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In VCT EMEA Stage 1 Group Alpha's Week 5 BO3, Natus Vincere (1-3, clinging to faint playoff hopes) meets winless Karmine Corp (0-4, eliminated) in a must-win for NAVI to force tiebreak scenarios alongside losses by Team Heretics and a Gentle Mates win over Team Liquid. NAVI's Week 4 1-2 loss to Team Liquid followed a 0-2 defeat to Gentle Mates, but new IGL chloric and stand-in ComeBack (filling for visa-delayed ExiT) bring added structure after an opening win over Heretics. Karmine Corp, after benching sheydos for N4RRATE and Avez shifting to IGL, stole a map from FUT Esports last week but remains map differential -27. Head-to-head favors NAVI from Kickoff, though both squads struggle in the current meta.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-12 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Mike Bost secured the Republican nomination unopposed in the March 17, 2026, Illinois primary for the safely Republican 12th Congressional District, facing Democrat Julie Fortier, who also ran without opposition. Trader consensus at 93.5% for Republicans reflects the district's strong GOP lean in southern Illinois, Bost's history of comfortable victories—including wide margins in recent cycles—and lack of competitive polling or fundraising signals favoring Democrats amid national generic ballot trends showing only modest Democratic edges elsewhere. Absent a major scandal, health event for Bost, or unprecedented turnout surge in this rural battleground, the seat remains a low-risk Republican hold ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Arouca qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Conference League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Conference League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Conference League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or the Primeira Liga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will ForecastEx self-certify sports event contracts by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Designated contract markets (DCMs) can self-certify sports event contracts with the CFTC under core principles, enabling binary outcome bets on games and scores without prior approval. CME Group self-certified in late 2025, launching via a new segment, while Kalshi and ForecastEx followed earlier that year, driving trader interest in compliant prediction markets. The CFTC's March 12 advisory heightened scrutiny, urging pre-certification talks, league data-sharing MOUs like MLB's, and anti-manipulation surveillance using official feeds. A Third Circuit ruling on April 6 affirmed federal preemption over state gambling laws for Kalshi's contracts. With two months to June 30, 2026, watch CFTC filings from Railbird or Aristotle for volume surges.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin Group D race atop the FIFA World Cup standings, with USA and Türkiye trading slim edges at 37% and 35% implied probabilities to win the group and secure direct knockout advancement alongside the runner-up. USA's co-host home advantage across venues like SoFi Stadium tempers recent stumbles, including heavy friendly losses to Belgium (5-2) and Portugal (2-0) earlier this month that exposed defensive frailties and Christian Pulisic's form dip amid high expectations under Mauricio Pochettino. Türkiye surged into contention via playoff triumphs over Romania and Kosovo on April 1, ending a 24-year World Cup absence with Hakan Çalhanoğlu's midfield mastery and young stars like Arda Güler fueling 17 goals in six qualifiers. Paraguay's CONMEBOL-honed defensive grit under Gustavo Alfaro (17%) and Australia's physical Socceroos pressing (7%) ensure no easy points in the expanded 12-group format, keeping advancement scenarios tight through head-to-heads and goal difference tiebreakers.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Guangzhou be 32°C or higher on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/guangzhou/ZGGG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Guangzhou Observatory's latest forecast, issued April 29, projects a May 1 high of 28°C under partly cloudy skies, anchoring trader consensus with 28.5% implied probability for that outcome, edging out 29°C (27.5%) and 27°C (25%). This reflects a warming trend from April 29's 26°C and April 30's 27°C peaks, fueled by a strengthening subtropical ridge over southern China and moderating sea breezes from the South China Sea. Differentiating factors include solar insolation levels—fuller sunshine could push toward 29°C via enhanced surface heating—versus increased humidity or afternoon convection capping at 27°C. Ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF show tight spreads around 27-29°C, underscoring short-range uncertainty; monitor hourly updates from official stations for resolution.

This marks a cooling trajectory from past records above 60 million, amid slower upload cadence and no announced blockbuster stunts like the ongoing Alaska challenge filming. With the prior April 30 market resolving "No," skin-in-the-game bettors see high barriers without viral pre-release hype; an upset would require a surprise mega-production or cross-platform buzz spiking initial velocity before month's end.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

The latest AtlasIntel survey (April 22–27) narrowed Lula's lead to 6.9 points, boosting Flávio's position as he consolidates the right-wing base with his father's endorsement amid economic headwinds pressuring Lula's support. No candidate nears 50% for an outright win, setting up a likely runoff; upcoming candidate registrations and May polls could shift dynamics among lower-tier contenders like Fernando Haddad.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 100-119 tweets from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 28 12:00 PM ET to May 5, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Park Soo-hyun holds a commanding 91% implied probability as the Democratic Party of Korea candidate in the June 3 Chungcheongnam Province gubernatorial election, driven by her April 15 primary runoff win over former Governor Yang Seung-jo and recent polls showing double-digit leads over incumbent People Power Party Governor Kim Tae-heum, such as the April 21 TJB survey (42.2% vs. 29.5% suitability) and an April 12 poll (50% vs. 30.9% head-to-head). Democratic Party's regional support exceeding 50% in polls, amid national opposition strength, underpins trader confidence despite Kim's economic pledges like Silicon Valley-style development. An upset remains possible via scandals, a late incumbent surge in battlegrounds like Cheonan and Asan, or shifts before early voting.

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton, married since 1975, will not announce a divorce by June 30, 2026, driven by the complete absence of credible filings, official statements, or verified marital discord in recent months. Periodic tabloid rumors—fueled by resurfaced Jeffrey Epstein documents from late 2025—have repeatedly failed to materialize into action, as evidenced by the couple's continued joint public appearances and Hillary's recent social media anniversary tribute affirming their 50-year union. With no fresh catalysts like legal proceedings or health crises emerging in the past 30 days, markets price in overwhelming stability; only a sudden, unforeseen announcement or scandal could shift odds before resolution.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump between April 1 and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's confirmed phone call with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on April 26, discussing transatlantic trade negotiations, NATO security commitments, and fallout from the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident, has anchored trader consensus amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz blockade and Ukraine ceasefire prospects. Reports of additional bilateral talks, including with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu around April 24 and Russian President Putin on April 29 covering Ukraine de-escalation and Iran, highlight active diplomacy, though unlisted in market outcomes. Lower probabilities for figures like Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Elon Musk, or Reza Pahlavi reflect no verified interactions despite regional volatility; the market closes April 30, 11:59 PM ET, with late announcements possible.

markets.question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $2,500 on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $300 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Apple (AAPL) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AAPL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.
